title: Particles: Politeness
description: Add sentence-final particles to sound polite and natural
intro: Vietnamese uses small words at the end of sentences to show respect, politeness, and tone. Without them, you sound robotic or rude. These particles are one of the most important things to master early.

questions.prompt: Which particle makes a sentence respectful to elders? 'Cảm ơn ___'
questions.explanation: 'ạ' shows respect — use it when speaking to anyone older or in a formal situation: 'Cảm ơn ạ' (Thank you, respectfully)

questions.prompt: Which particle is used for gentle suggestions? 'Đi ngủ ___' (Let's go to sleep)
questions.explanation: 'nhé' softens a suggestion or request, like saying 'okay?' — 'Đi ngủ nhé' = Let's go to sleep, okay?
Always add 'ạ' when greeting someone older or more senior: 'Xin chào ạ'
'nha' is the Southern/casual version of 'nhé': 'Đi ngủ nha' = Let's sleep, yeah?

questions.prompt: Mời anh ngồi ___ (Please sit down — to an older man)
questions.explanation: When offering something to someone older, always end with 'ạ' for respect

questions.prompt: Which particle is a direct command softener? 'Ăn ___ !' (Eat!)
questions.explanation: 'đi' at the end softens an imperative: 'Ăn đi' = Go ahead and eat. Without 'đi' it sounds harsh

questions.prompt: Cho tôi một ly cà phê ___ (One coffee, please — to a waiter)
questions.explanation: In a café, you'd be polite to the staff: 'Cho tôi một ly cà phê ạ'

questions.prompt: Which ending would you use with a close friend? 'Đi ăn ___' (Let's go eat)
questions.explanation: 'nha' is casual and friendly — perfect for peers: 'Đi ăn nha' = Let's go eat, yeah?

questions.prompt: Dạ, em hiểu rồi ___ (Yes, I understand — to a teacher)
questions.explanation: 'Dạ...ạ' is the full respectful frame: 'Dạ' at the start + 'ạ' at the end
'vâng' (North) and 'dạ' (South) both mean 'yes' respectfully. 'dạ' is also used in the North before 'ạ'

questions.prompt: Ngon lắm ___ ! (So delicious! — to the cook, who is older)
questions.explanation: Complimenting someone older → add 'ạ' for respect

questions.prompt: You're at a market. The vendor is about your parents' age. How do you say 'How much?'
questions.explanation: To someone older → 'ạ'. 'nhé' would be to a peer, 'đi' is for commands, 'nha' is too casual here

questions.prompt: Chờ tôi một chút ___ (Wait for me a moment — to a friend)
questions.explanation: 'nhé' for gentle requests between peers: 'Wait for me a moment, okay?'

questions.prompt: Which is the most natural way to tell a friend to try the food?
questions.explanation: 'đi' is the best particle for encouraging someone to do something: 'Go ahead and try it!'

questions.prompt: Tôi đi trước ___ (I'll go first — to a friend, casually)
questions.explanation: Casual notification to a friend → 'nha': 'I'll head out first, yeah?'

questions.correct: When speaking to anyone older than you
options: When speaking to anyone older than you
questions.explanation: 'ạ' is expected whenever you talk to someone older — parents, teachers, shopkeepers, strangers who look older. It's never wrong to use it
